methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Forge Nano is a Series C-stage climate and energy company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage private companies: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

Two methods were applied to triangulate fair value. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $40M total raised as a baseline proxy, yielding $68M. The peer-median method anchors to the $1.0B median across the three comparable Series C climate-tech companies, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. The wide dispersion among peers—spanning a 2.9x range—reflects genuine variance in technology maturity, market timing, and capital efficiency within the frontier climate sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $58M (floor, conservative stage-based method) to $1.1B (ceiling, high-end peer multiple). The median implied value of $534M reflects tension between the company's early capital base and peer-set positioning. Key uncertainties include commercialization timeline, relative technology differentiation versus KoBold and Electric Hydrogen, and whether Forge Nano's capital efficiency justifies positioning closer to the $700M–$1.0B band or the lower $68M–$200M band. Additional diligence on unit economics and customer traction would narrow this range materially.
